name: Wine Loop
description:
On this circular route, the Llobregat and Cardener rivers act as main guides, accompanying you throughout the journey. The adventure includes a climb to the top of the mountain, where the ancient town of Castelladral awaits you, a perfect place to take a break and enjoy the panoramic views from 652 metres high. In addition, if you are a wine lover, you have several opportunities to enjoy delicious tastings at local wineries, adding a special touch to your experience on this charming journey.

The route covers approximately 61 kilometres, with a cumulative positive gradient of 810 metres. To make the most of the experience, we recommend having an intermediate physical level, as the journey can be quite demanding. 

The roads used on the Route are in good condition and are generally quiet, although during rush hour and on holidays you may encounter a higher volume of traffic.
region_intro: Road cycling around Sallent offers diverse terrain within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by high-mountain landscapes and significant elevation changes. The region features prominent peaks, glacial lakes, and reservoirs like Lanuza, with the Gállego and Aguas Limpias rivers flowing through forests and pastures. This mountainous environment provides a network of road cycling routes, ranging from challenging climbs to more moderate loops. Best road cycling routes around Sallent The most popular road cycling route is Wine Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers expansive views of the Pyrenean landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Feixes de la Sala loop from Sallent, a moderate 20.1 miles (32.3 km) path. This route winds through the natural environment surrounding Sallent, showcasing the Tena Valley. Local road cyclists also love the PC St. Joan de Vilatorrada – Sant Sadurní Church (Callús) loop from Sallent, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Road cycling around Sallent is defined by high-mountain landscapes, glacial lakes, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides around reservoirs to difficult ascents. The routes in Sallent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 17,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sallent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sallent?
A: Sallent offers a comprehensive network of over 280 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to very challenging ascents, allowing every cyclist to find a suitable adventure in the Aragonese Pyrenees.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Sallent?
A: Yes, Sallent provides several easy and moderate options suitable for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22.5 km route. The region also features a nearly flat tarmac road connecting Sallent to Lanuza, perfect for a relaxed ride with scenic views of the Lanuza Reservoir.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes near Sallent?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Sallent is renowned for its demanding ascents. The 27.8 km route to Col du Portalet offers stunning Pyrenean views and significant elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from la Botjosa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 75.3 km with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. The Cotefablo Pass loop also presents a substantial challenge with 2,040 meters of elevation gain over 107 km.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Sallent?
A: The best time for road cycling in Sallent is typically from May to October. During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and high-mountain passes are usually free of snow, making for safer and more enjoyable rides. Autumn also offers a spectacular display of colors in the interior sierras of Partacua and Tendeñera.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Sallent?
A: Road cycling around Sallent immerses you in dramatic high-mountain Pyrenean scenery. You'll encounter peaks exceeding 3,000 meters, pristine glacial lakes known as 'ibones,' and prominent reservoirs like Lanuza. The routes often follow the Gállego and Aguas Limpias rivers, winding through rich natural environments of forests and pastures, offering breathtaking vistas throughout your ride.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Sallent?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Sallent are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1815967217/wine-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 60.7 km circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Feixes de la Sala loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.3 km moderate path that winds through the natural environment surrounding Sallent.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several cultural and historical attractions. The town of Sallent itself features a charming city center with a Gothic church and a medieval bridge. Nearby, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1096822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or the scenic Lanuza reservoir, which also hosts the Southern Pyrenees Music Festival annually. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talamanca Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3826689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Vell de Manresa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sallent?
A: The road cycling routes in Sallent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to scenic loops around reservoirs, and the stunning high-mountain Pyrenean landscapes that make every ride memorable.

Q: Are there routes that offer significant elevation gain for training?
A: Absolutely. Sallent is an excellent destination for cyclists looking for significant elevation gain. Routes like the one to Col du Portalet are known for their substantial climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from la Botjosa&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features over 1,100 meters of ascent, providing a rigorous workout amidst spectacular scenery.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through villages or offer opportunities for refreshments?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, many routes, especially those that are circular or pass through populated areas, will offer opportunities to stop in villages for refreshments. The region's natural environment and charming towns provide a pleasant backdrop for breaks during your ride.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for road cycling routes in Sallent?
A: Distances and elevation gains vary widely. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt;, can be around 22.5 km with 250 meters of elevation. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1815967217/wine-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are about 60 km with 800 meters of climbing. Difficult routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from la Botjosa&lt;/a&gt;, can exceed 75 km with over 1,100 meters of elevation, and some even reach over 100 km with 2,000+ meters of gain.
tips: Church of the municipality of Callús (Bages) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia. This church was built in 1936 due to the fact that at the end of the 19th century a series of textile industries were installed on the banks of the Cardener River that were a pole of attraction for the population; Thus, a new town was set up on the plain that was far from the parish church located on top of a hill near the castle of Callús.
It's a good place to take a break. They have a special breakfast for cyclists.
© Balsareny Castle is located on a small hill that controls the Llobregat valley.
